title: cistus polymorphus subvar. leiocarpus
intro: Cistus polymorphus subvar. leiocarpus, also known as rockrose or labdanum, is a plant species within the Cistus genus, characterized by its white or pale pink flowers and thick, leathery leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oil, which contains compounds such as labdanic acid and phenolic acids, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of cistus polymorphus subvar. leiocarpus include its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and protect against infections, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for wound care and skin issues. Cistus polymorphus subvar. leiocarpus can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and essential oils, which are often used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
